action.reasoning: Observation: The current predict.py on disk is corrupted/truncated, causing catastrophic eval scores (~0.22) and smoke-test failures on all map types. The best-ever score is 0.6118 (iteration 7), but the most reproducible strong baseline is ~0.607 from a clean global greedy planner (utility/(dist+3.6)). With ~32 minutes and 5 iterations left, I must first re-establish a working, complete predict.py and confirm the baseline score. Analysis: The failures are clearly due to incomplete code, not algorithmic weakness. I will completely overwrite predict.py with a concise, fully self-contained SimpleGreedyPlanner using only proven mechanisms: global Dijkstra, utility/(dist+3.6), anti-backtracking penalty (0.3), and a simple fallback. I will also overwrite train.py and smoke_test.py to match. After a quick smoke test, I will immediately submit evaluation. Decision: Write clean, complete predict.py / train.py / smoke_test.py, run train + smoke test, then submit eval to measure the restored baseline.
